title: Error Handling & Fatal Error Recovery
description: Catching, reporting, and recovering from PHP fatal errors inside instrumented applications. Covers two complementary systems: (1) Laravel/Lumen Illuminate Exception Handler for standard exception capture/rendering, and (2) WordPress WP_Fatal_Error_Handler for PHP shutdown-time fatal error recovery (WSOD protection). Both operate within Datadog PHP tracer-instrumented applications.

use_cases.name: Catch & Report Exception
use_cases.notes: Delegates to parent Illuminate ExceptionHandler::report(); skips types listed in $dontReport

use_cases.name: Render Exception as HTTP Response
use_cases.notes: Delegates to parent::render(); returns HTTP response for the exception

use_cases.name: Register Custom Exception Reporting Callbacks
use_cases.notes: Calls $this->reportable(Closure). Used in v8+ and v10 Handler variants.

use_cases.name: Suppress Specific Exception Types from Reporting
use_cases.notes: Laravel v10 adds IgnoredException::class. Parent class checks this before logging.

use_cases.name: Suppress Sensitive Input from Session Flash
use_cases.notes: Prevents password / password_confirmation / current_password from being flashed on validation exceptions.

use_cases.name: Detect PHP Fatal Error on Shutdown
use_cases.notes: Registered via register_shutdown_function(). Bails if WP_SANDBOX_SCRAPING or wp_is_maintenance_mode().

use_cases.name: Filter Handleable PHP Error Types
use_cases.notes: Handles E_ERROR, E_PARSE, E_USER_ERROR, E_COMPILE_ERROR, E_RECOVERABLE_ERROR. Extensible via wp_should_handle_php_error filter.

use_cases.name: Recover via WordPress Recovery Mode
use_cases.notes: Only on non-multisite when recovery mode is initialized. Sets $handled = true on success.

use_cases.name: Display Custom PHP Error Template
use_cases.notes: Drop-in php-error.php controls HTTP status code and HTML. If absent, falls back to UC10.

use_cases.name: Display Default Fatal Error Page (wp_die)
use_cases.notes: Response 500. Message varies: recovery mode active, protected endpoint (single/multisite), or generic. Filtered by wp_php_error_message and wp_php_error_args.

use_cases.name: Override Fatal Error Handler via Drop-in
use_cases.notes: Custom class can override handle(), detect_error(), should_handle_error(), display_error_template(), display_default_error_template() selectively.

use_cases.name: Skip Fatal Handling in Sandbox / Maintenance Mode
use_cases.method: handle(): early return if WP_SANDBOX_SCRAPING || wp_is_maintenance_mode()
use_cases.notes: Prevents interference during plugin/theme updates and automated scraping.

key_relationships: Handler uses $this->reportable() to register Datadog/custom reporting closures
wp_filters_hooks: wp_should_handle_php_error — extend which error types are handled
wp_php_error_message — customize the user-facing fatal error HTML message
wp_php_error_args — customize wp_die() arguments (response code, links)
flow_summary: handle() checks sandbox/maintenance mode guards → calls detect_error()
detect_error() calls error_get_last() → passes to should_handle_error()
should_handle_error() checks error type list + wp_should_handle_php_error filter
If error actionable: try wp_recovery_mode()->handle_error($error) on non-multisite
display_error_template(): load php-error.php drop-in OR call display_default_error_template()
display_default_error_template(): build message (recovery/admin/generic), apply filters, wp_die(WP_Error, '', {response:500})
Laravel request lifecycle throws exception → ExceptionHandler catches it
Handler::render() converts exception to HTTP response
Handler::register() wires up custom reportable() callbacks (e.g. Datadog tracer hooks)
important_notes: This project is the Datadog PHP distributed tracing library. The Handler and WP_Fatal_Error_Handler are test-framework fixtures used to validate that Datadog's auto-instrumentation correctly captures exceptions/fatal errors as span error events (ExceptionSpanEvent) without disrupting the host framework's own error handling pipeline. The WP_Fatal_Error_Handler uses PHP shutdown functions (not exceptions), making it a distinct code path from the Illuminate Handler.
